verdict: The subglacial hydraulic potential phi is NOT a Leray pressure. The Leray transfer H_p=1/k^2 is real and omega-independent (max phase 0.0e+00, instantaneous), while the Darcy head transfer H_phi=1/(k^2+i*omega/D_h) is screened (|H_phi|<|H_p|) and lags the forcing by arctan(omega/(D_h k^2)): the dominant mode lags 0.785 rad = 45 deg at its hydraulic clock omega_c=D_h k^2 (NR28 p/phi crossover). A driven test recovers the head lag 0.785 rad (closed form 0.785) while the Leray pressure lag is -2.0e-06 (none). The two operators coincide ONLY as D_h->inf (tau_hyd->0): the relative gap falls 0.71->1.0e-04 over the sweep. So phi has storage, a finite hydraulic time (tau_hyd~1.17 d here) and memory; the Leray pressure has none. The falsifiable separator is a nonzero measured hydraulic response time.
